title: notelaea microcarpa var. microcarpa
intro: Notelaea microcarpa var. microcarpa, also known as the Small-fruited Notelaea, is a rare and endemic shrub native to eastern Australia. It is a type of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, known for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The leaves and roots of this plant are used in herbal preparations, traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. When used in tea or tincture form, Notelaea microcarpa var. microcarpa is believed to have potential health benefits, including soothing respiratory issues and supporting immune function.
